notes: Two-rule fixture: (1) TQ-1 concessive 'mah' on subject pronoun, null-target — NMT itself produces the 'as for X' rendering naturally; (2) TQ-3 gender-neutral 'dia → they'. 'selalu telat' = 'always late'. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'They are always late' (no 'as for them') could be more natural depending on conversational context; the mah-induced topicalization is sometimes lost in casual English.
